papers.title: An Intelligent Fault Diagnosis Method for General Aviation Aircraft Based on Multi-Fidelity Digital Twin and FMEA Knowledge Enhancement

papers.abstract:
arXiv:2604.22777v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Fault diagnosis of general aviation aircraft faces challenges including scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. This paper proposes an intelligent fault diagnosis framework based on multi-fidelity digital twin, integrating four modules: high-fidelity flight dynamics simulation, FMEA-driven fault injection,...

papers.title: The Power of Power Law: Asymmetry Enables Compositional Reasoning

Abstract: Natural language data follows a power-law distribution, with most knowledge and skills appearing at very low frequency. While a common intuition suggests that reweighting or curating data towards a uniform distribution may help models better learn these long-tail skills, we find a counterintuitive result: across a wide range of compositional...

Abstract: Preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs) extend Dung's approach to abstract argumentation (AAFs) by encoding preferences over arguments. Such preferences control the transformation of attacks into defeats, and different approaches to doing so result in different reductions from a PAF to an AAF. In this paper we consider a PAF inverse...

To address this, we introduce Analytica, a novel agent architecture built on the principle of Soft...

papers.title: Independent, academic cancer trials are vital to improve patient outcomes worldwide
papers.authors: Medical Xpress
papers.abstract: Independent, academic clinical cancer trials are essential to improving patient outcomes, reducing inequalities in care, and strengthening health care systems worldwide, according to a new initiative published in The Lancet Oncology. The paper, "Academic clinical cancer trials to improve patient outcomes," announces the launch of a Lancet Oncology...

papers.title: Deterioration in cognitive function precedes cardiovascular disease events in seniors
papers.authors: Medical Xpress
papers.abstract: For older adults, deterioration in cognitive function is seen prior to cardiovascular disease (CVD) events, according to a study published online April 20 in JAMA Network Open.

papers.title: Statement highlights how brain health is shaped by lifetime mental, physical, environmental and lifestyle factors
papers.authors: Medical Xpress
papers.abstract: Brain health isn't determined only by genetics or what happens later in life. A growing body of research shows that a range of factors—from mental health and sleep to the environment, lifestyle and social conditions—play a powerful role in shaping how the brain functions and ages.

papers.title: How the immune system battles lifelong viral infections acquired at birth
papers.authors: Medical Xpress
papers.abstract: Millions of people worldwide carry viral infections they acquired at birth, often for life. For a long time it was assumed that the immune system hardly fights these pathogens. Researchers from the University of Basel show now that the body's defenses do indeed act against the virus. This could be a useful starting point for future therapies.

papers.title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
papers.authors: Connie Loizos
papers.abstract: It's a story Musk has told before -- in interviews and to author Walter Isaacson for his bestselling biography of Musk -- but Tuesday was the first time he said it under oath.

papers.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
papers.authors: Julie Bort
papers.abstract: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

papers.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
papers.authors: Lauren Forristal
papers.abstract: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

papers.title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
papers.authors: Julie Bort
papers.abstract: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

papers.title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
papers.authors: Sarah Perez
papers.abstract: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

papers.title: PRMT7 restricts CD8+ T cells expansion via the NF-κB pathway.
papers.authors: Srour, N.; Luo, K.; Allard, D.; Xiong, Y.; Yu, Z.; Papadopoulos, T.; Santinon, F.; Hsieh, C.-H.; Stagg, J.; Barsyte-Lovejoy, D.; del Rincon, S. V.; Jin, J.; Richard, S.
papers.abstract: Abstract New therapeutics are needed to enhance cytotoxic T lymphocyte (CTL) expansion and effector function for effective tumor control. Here, we show that T cell specific deletion of Prmt7 using CD4-Cre mice increases CD8+ effector differentiation, cytokine secretion, cytolytic activity, and anti-tumor responses. Prmt7 deficiency transcriptionally reprogrammed CD8+ T cells by activating the...

papers.title: Whole-genome sequencing of a mid-20th-century femur from central Israel in an open missing-person case
papers.authors: Vol, E.; Waldman, S.; Lomes, A.; Brielle, E. S.; Appel, N.; Dolin, B.; Asif, S.; Nagar, Y.; Marco, E.; Bergman, N.; Khaner, O.; Raviv, D.; Oliel, J.; Lewis, R. Y.; Carmi, S.
papers.abstract: Genome-wide technologies can generate investigative leads in cold cases by determining the genetic ancestry of the forensic sample. Increasingly, DNA extraction and whole-genome sequencing or genotyping are being used to analyze early or middle-20th century skeletal remains. Here, we present the first case, to our knowledge, of whole-genome sequencing of a middle-20th-century bone sample from the...

papers.title: Population analysis and host-disease associations of Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli from various sources across eleven European countries using whole genome sequencing
papers.authors: Tozzoli, R.; Schadron, T.; Knijn, A.; De Sabato, L.; Morabito, S.; Montalbano Di Filippo, M.; Fiskebeck, E.; Johannessen, G.; Antony-Samy, J. K.; Good, L.; Soderlund, R.; van Hoek, A.; Mughini Gras, L.; Franz, E.; Wieczorek, K.; Scavia, G.; Moro, O.; Chiani, P.; Michelacci, V.; Burgess, C. M.; Duffy, G.; Rodgers, J.; Kirchner, M.; Pista, A.; Silveira, L.; Amaro, A.; Clemente, L.; Chattaway, M. A.; Pires, S
papers.abstract: Shiga toxin-producing Escherichia coli (STEC) are important foodborne pathogens, able to cause severe disease in humans. In the DiSCoVeR project (https://onehealthejp.eu/jrp-discover/) a STEC inventory from human and non-human sources from 11 European countries was set up and [≥] 3500 strains were sequenced to perform comparative genomics analysis. We used this dataset to assess STEC...

papers.title: Connecting polygenic disease risk to cell states and regulatory programs through single-cell chromatin accessibility
papers.authors: Yu, L.; Deary, L. T.; Liu, Q.; Zhang, Q.; Zhao, S.
papers.abstract: Single-cell ATAC-sequencing enables high-resolution mapping of cell states and their putative regulatory elements. The enrichment of genome-wide association study (GWAS) signals within context-specific regulatory regions can reveal disease-relevant cell populations. We present Single-Cell ATAC-seq Disease Score (SCADS), a computational framework that integrates GWAS summary statistics with...

papers.title: Chromosome-scale genome assemblies of duckweeds provide insights into genomic plasticity, aquatic adaptation and morphological reduction
papers.authors: Tek, M. I.; Boyle, B.; Normandeau, E.; Lecomte, L.; Sabri, M.; Torkamaneh, D.
papers.abstract: Duckweeds (Lemnaceae) present a striking example of convergent genome evolution following the return from land to water. As the smallest and fastest-growing angiosperms, they exhibit extreme morphological reduction yet retain remarkable genomic plasticity through recurrent interspecific hybridisation, chromosomal rearrangements, and selective gene-family remodelling. The genomic mechanisms that...

papers.title: Optimal Reference Panel Design in Ancient DNA Imputation from Coalescent Theory, Simulation, and Real Data Application with an Ancient Reference Panel
papers.authors: Sousa da Mota, B.; Kumar, K.; Reich, D. E.; Zoellner, S.
papers.abstract: Imputation is widely used in the ancient DNA (aDNA) field to determine which phenotypically important alleles ancient individuals carried, to study natural selection, and to detect segments of the genome that are shared between individuals identical by descent. However, rare variant imputation is less accurate, and rare variants tend to be excluded from downstream analyses. State-of-the-art...

papers.title: Genome-wide association study of morphometric and metabolic characteristics in the European populations of the sugar kelp Saccharina latissima
papers.authors: MAUGER, S.; AVIA, K.; JAUGEON, L.; RUGGERI, P.; NEHR, Z.; SALIA, O. I.; COUDRET, J.; GOUHIER, E.; BAUD, A.; LOISEL, S.; FORT, A.; SULPICE, R.; DESTOMBE, C.; POTIN, P.; COCK, J. M.; VALERO, M.
papers.abstract: The sugar kelp Saccharina latissima is a promising candidate for sustainable aquaculture in the North Atlantic and North-East Pacific but genetic improvement has been hindered by limited understanding of the genetic basis of economically important traits. We conducted the first genome-wide association study (GWAS) for this species using 202 self-fertilised pseudo-F1 individuals derived from 12...

papers.title: Fetal exposure to paracetamol is associated with altered markers of ovarian development and reduced uterine volume in girls: the COPANA study
papers.authors: Fischer, M. B.; Mola, G.; Sundberg, K.; Scheel, L.; Wraae, K. B.; Rom, A. L.; Frederiksen, H.; Anderson, R. A.; Assens, M.; Andersson, A.-M.; Priskorn, L.; Petersen, J. H.; Hegaard, H. K.; Main, K. M.; Kristensen, D. M.; Juul, A.; Hagen, C. P.
papers.abstract: Study question; is fetal exposure to paracetamol associated with markers of ovarian function in infancy? Summary answer; Mild to moderate doses of prenatal paracetamol exposure, assessed by detailed maternal reports and urinary measurements, is associated with ovarian morphology and activity as well as reduced size of estrogen-responsive tissues in infant girls. What is known already; Maternal...

papers.title: Spatiotemporal Tau Accumulation and Its Clinical Impact on PSP: Longitudinal Florzolotau (18F) PET
papers.authors: Chishiki, Y.; Tagai, K.; Kataoka, Y.; Goto, R.; Osawa, K.; Oyama, A.; Matsumoto, H.; Ichihashi, M.; Momota, Y.; Kamada, T.; Seki, C.; Matsuoka, K.; Hirata, K.; Kurose, S.; Moriguchi, S.; Komatsu, Y.; Kato, H.; Yamamoto, Y.; Nakano, Y.; Hirano, S.; Shinoto, H.; Shimada, H.; Tokuda, T.; Kawamura, K.; Zhang, M.-R.; Takahata, K.; Higuchi, M.; Endo, H.
papers.abstract: [Background] Florzolotau (18F) positron emission tomography (florzolotau PET) enables high-contrast in vivo detection of four-repeat tau pathology in progressive supranuclear palsy (PSP), but whether longitudinal tau imaging reflects disease progression remains unclear. [Objectives] To explore longitudinal tau accumulation using florzolotau PET and evaluate its association with clinical...

papers.title: A Hybrid Framework for Accurate Melanoma Diagnosis: Leveraging Generative AI with Enhanced CNN+ Architectures
papers.authors: Wu, Y.; Zhang, B.; Yan, Y.; Li, J.; Wu, Y.; Kim, S. S.; Huang, K.; Ye, Q.; Yu, Y.; Tong, G.
papers.abstract: Melanocytes become cancerous, forming tumors that may invade and destroy the surrounding tissues. When melanocytes acquire invasive characteristics, the anchored melanoma begins to damage the normal cells. Therefore, early intervention and diagnosis are essential to avoid high morbidity and mortality in malignant melanoma. However, It is challenging to distinguish the difference between malignant...

papers.title: Co-expressed MicroRNAs Associated with An Elevated Psychometabolic Risk Phenotype in Women during Midlife
papers.authors: Longoria, K. D.; Stroebel, B.; Gadgil, M.; Perez, N.; Lewis, K. A.; Weiss, S. J.; Flowers, E.
papers.abstract: Introduction The bidirectional relationship between depression and type 2 diabetes (T2D) is well-established. Women are disproportionately affected by their co-occurrence, particularly during midlife, yet sex- and age-specific studies on phenotypic and mechanistic factors underlying risk for their co-occurrence are limited. The purpose of this study was to identify combined risk profiles (i.e.,...

papers.title: Reconciling neurocognitive and behavioral impulsivities through ecological assessment and multivariate modelling of cognitive control dynamics
papers.authors: imparato, a.; Reich, N.; Riviere, G.; Eliez, S.; Graser, C.; Schneider, M.; Sandini, C.
papers.abstract: Impulsivity is a core dimension of ADHD and a transdiagnostic vulnerability factor for a wide range of adverse psychiatric and somatic outcomes, that could be mitigated through more effective screening of at-risk individuals. However, laboratory-based measures of impulsivity show weak convergence across paradigms and limited prediction of real-world behavior, constraining their utility. We tested...

papers.title: Identifying autism in women diagnosed with borderline personality disorder: Clinician and lived experience perspectives.
papers.authors: Parker, J. A.; Thompson, E.; Mandy, W.; McCabe, R.; Stark, E.; Barnicot, K.
papers.abstract: Background. Growing numbers of people with a borderline personality disorder (BPD) diagnosis are realising they may have undiagnosed autism. Previous qualitative research has not focused on identifying barriers and facilitators to this diagnostic journey, did not explore the perspectives of clinicians, and did not include the experiences of people who are unsure whether they are autistic or not....

papers.title: Lactoferrin and lysozyme for Kenyan children presenting with wasting and diarrhea: A 2 x 2 factorial randomized controlled trial
papers.authors: Tickell, K. D.; Tiwari, R.; Trehan, I.; Otieno, J.; Okello, M.; Shah, A.; Keter, L.; Yoshioka, E.; Ochola, E.; Nyabinda, C.; Rwigi, D.; Njunge, J. M.; Houpt, E.; Platts-Mills, J. A.; Liu, J.; Richardson, B. A.; McGrath, C. J.; Means, A. R.; Diakhate, M. M.; Stewart, G. J.; Walson, J. L.; Singa, B. O.; Pavlinac, P. B.
papers.abstract: Introduction: Lactoferrin and lysozyme are milk derived proteins with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. We tested if these supplements improved time-to-nutritional-recovery and reduced the incidence of new moderate or severe diarrhea (MSD) among children presenting to hospital with wasting and diarrhea. Methods: Medically-stable children aged 6-24 months with diarrhea and wasting...

papers.title: Author Correction: Quantum error correction below the surface code threshold
papers.authors: Nicholas Zobrist
papers.abstract: Nature, Published online: 28 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10559-8 Author Correction: Quantum error correction below the surface code threshold

papers.title: The equity paradox of environmental DNA for biodiversity monitoring

papers.abstract: Nature, Published online: 28 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01349-3 The equity paradox of environmental DNA for biodiversity monitoring

papers.title: To hire good scientists, look at their peer-reviewing records
papers.authors: David Comerford
papers.abstract: Nature, Published online: 28 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01368-0 To hire good scientists, look at their peer-reviewing records

papers.title: Gulf states must move from efficiency to resilience
papers.authors: Abrar Al-Hasan
papers.abstract: Nature, Published online: 28 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01366-2 Gulf states must move from efficiency to resilience

papers.title: The politics of playful primates
papers.authors: Nature News
papers.abstract: Nature, Published online: 28 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01352-8 Apes, monkeys and their relatives that live in despotic societies are less likely to clown around as adults.

papers.title: Fauci aide indicted over federal records violations related to COVID-19
papers.authors: Jon Cohen
papers.abstract: David Morens could face prison time for allegedly concealing back-channel efforts to help nonprofit accused of starting pandemic

papers.title: Famed Brazilian dinosaur fossil finally heads home
papers.authors: Taylor Mitchell BrownRodrigo Pérez Ortega
papers.abstract: Germany and Brazil reach agreement over controversial spinosaurid fossil, heralding new collaboration between the two nations

papers.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
papers.authors: OpenAI Blog
papers.abstract: OpenAI GPT models, Codex, and Managed Agents are now available on AWS, enabling enterprises to build secure AI in their AWS environments.

papers.title: Our commitment to community safety
papers.authors: OpenAI Blog
papers.abstract: Learn how OpenAI protects community safety in ChatGPT through model safeguards, misuse detection, policy enforcement, and collaboration with safety experts.

papers.title: High-resolution global recombination mapping in C. elegans reveals sexual dimorphisms shaped by meiotic chromosomal features and structures
papers.authors: Bush, Z. D.; Conery, J. S.; Wilson, H. R.; Naftaly, A. F.; Dinwiddie, D.; Hillers, K. J.; Libuda, D. E.
papers.abstract: Crossover recombination events during meiosis repair double-strand DNA breaks and ensure accurate chromosome segregation in most organisms. For many species, the genomic distribution of crossovers is nonrandom and sexually dimorphic. While many species evolved kilobase-scale 'hotspots' for crossover formation, the Caenorhabditis elegans genome lacks hotspots, and crossovers are enriched across...

papers.title: Human Gut Phageome Analysis Uncovers Thousands of Highly Modular Endolysins
papers.authors: Niloy, R. K.; Jewel, N. A.; Karim, D.; Rolin, M. H.; Khan, T.; Akter, A.; Mondal, S. I.
papers.abstract: The escalating threat of antimicrobial resistance has renewed global interest in bacteriophages as precise and powerful tools for controlling bacterial populations in the human gut. These viruses owe much of their antibacterial potential to phage-encoded endolysins, enzymes capable of rapidly degrading bacterial cell walls with high specificity and low potential for resistance development....

papers.title: Connecting multiway enhancer-promoter interactions to changes in gene expression in cancer
papers.authors: Kumari, K.; Shin, S.; Shi, G.; Reed, K. S.; Misteli, T.; Thirumalai, D.
papers.abstract: Hi-C, and more recently Micro-C, experiments suggest that genome organization is altered as normal cells become cancerous, often resulting in aberrant gene expression. In prostate cancer, there is evidence that large topologically associating domains in normal cells split and are accompanied by shifts in the epigenetic marks from inactive to active states. However, it is unclear whether changes...

papers.title: Bizarre Hawking radiation may smooth the jagged hearts of black holes
papers.authors: Adrian Cho
papers.abstract: Hawking’s signature prediction may prevent vexing singularities from forming

papers.title: How an HIV/AIDS tragedy spurred human evolution
papers.authors: Jon Cohen
papers.abstract: Before antiretroviral drugs reached South Africa, high death toll shaped immune system genes

papers.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
papers.authors: OpenAI Blog
papers.abstract: OpenAI is available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling secure AI adoption for U.S. federal agencies.

papers.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
papers.authors: OpenAI Blog
papers.abstract: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended agreement that simplifies the partnership, adds long-term clarity, and supports continued AI innovation at scale.

papers.title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
papers.authors: OpenAI Blog
papers.abstract: Learn how Symphony, an open-source spec for Codex orchestration, turns issue trackers into always-on agent systems—boosting engineering output and reducing context switching.

papers.title: Trump fires all 24 members of the U.S. National Science Foundation’s governing body
papers.authors: Jeffrey Mervis
papers.abstract: Dismissal of the National Science Board is widely seen as latest move to erase NSF’s independence

papers.title: Turkana Food Inc. Recalls Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste 1lb (16oz) Because of Possible Health Risk
papers.authors: CDC News
papers.abstract: Turkana Food Inc. Kenilworth, NJ is recalling 858 cases OF Aleppo Tahini Sesame Paste because it has the potential to be contaminated with Salmonella, an organism which can cause serious and sometimes fatal infections in young children, frail or elderly people, and others with weakened immune system

papers.title: NASA confirms 2024 as hottest year on record, part of accelerating warming trend
papers.authors: NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies
papers.abstract: NASA and NOAA analyses confirm that 2024 was the warmest year since global records began in 1880, continuing a decade-long trend of accelerating global temperature rise driven by greenhouse gas emissions.

papers.title: Copernicus: 2024 is the first year to exceed 1.5C above pre-industrial levels
papers.authors: Copernicus Climate Change Service
papers.abstract: 2024 was the warmest year on record globally and the first year exceeding 1.5C above the pre-industrial average, according to ERA5 data from Copernicus Climate Change Service and World Meteorological Organization.

papers.title: OpenAI o3 and o3-mini: Pushing the boundaries of AI reasoning
papers.authors: OpenAI Research Team
papers.abstract: OpenAI announces o3 and o3-mini models, demonstrating significant advances in reasoning capabilities through deliberative alignment and chain-of-thought approaches, achieving breakthrough results on ARC-AGI benchmark.

papers.title: UNEP Emissions Gap Report 2024: No more hot air... please!
papers.authors: United Nations Environment Programme
papers.abstract: The 2024 Emissions Gap Report finds that current national climate commitments would reduce projected global warming to 2.6-2.8C, far above the Paris Agreement's 1.5C goal, urging immediate action.

papers.title: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof achieves silver-medal level in mathematical olympiad
papers.authors: DeepMind Team
papers.abstract: AlphaProof, a new AI system combining language models with reinforcement learning, has achieved silver-medal standard solving complex mathematical problems at the International Mathematical Olympiad level.

papers.title: Living with Long COVID
papers.authors: CDC News
papers.abstract: People with post-COVID conditions and those who support them can find ways to help, ways to cope with stress, and additional resources and support groups.

papers.title: Long COVID Basics
papers.authors: CDC News
papers.abstract: Learn about the potential COVID-19 long-term effects, including fatigue, shortness of breath, brain fog, chest pain, headache, and other symptoms.

papers.title: AlphaFold 3 predicts structures of nearly every molecule in biology
papers.authors: DeepMind / Isomorphic Labs
papers.abstract: Google DeepMind releases AlphaFold 3, expanding protein structure prediction to DNA, RNA, and small molecules, opening new avenues for drug discovery and biological research.

papers.title: COVID-19 Testing: What You Need to Know
papers.authors: CDC News
papers.abstract: Learn about testing for COVID-19, including the types of tests, who should get tested, how to get tested, and print resources in English and Spanish.

papers.title: Meta releases Llama 3: Most capable openly available LLM to date
papers.authors: Meta AI
papers.abstract: Meta announces Llama 3, the latest version of its open large language model, featuring significant improvements in reasoning, code generation, and multilingual capabilities across 8B and 70B parameter models.

papers.title: COVID-19 Vaccines for People Who Are Moderately or Severely Immunocompromised
papers.authors: CDC News
papers.abstract: Learn CDC recommendations for COVID-19 vaccines for moderately to severely immunocompromised people. An additional dose is recommended.

papers.title: CDC updates respiratory virus guidance for COVID-19, flu, and RSV
papers.authors: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
papers.abstract: The CDC issued updated respiratory virus guidance that brings a unified approach to addressing risks from COVID-19, flu, and RSV, emphasizing core prevention strategies.

papers.title: FDA approves first CRISPR-based gene editing therapy for sickle cell disease
papers.authors: U.S. Food and Drug Administration
papers.abstract: The FDA approved Casgevy, the first CRISPR/Cas9 genome-edited cell therapy for the treatment of sickle cell disease in patients 12 years and older.

papers.title: WHO declares end of COVID-19 as a global health emergency
papers.authors: World Health Organization
papers.abstract: The WHO Director-General declares the end of COVID-19 as a public health emergency of international concern (PHEIC), marking a transition in the global response to the pandemic.

papers.title: Hypertension: Key facts and latest statistics from WHO
papers.authors: World Health Organization
papers.abstract: Key facts about hypertension worldwide: 1.28 billion adults aged 30-79 years have hypertension, and two-thirds live in low- and middle-income countries. It is a major cause of premature death worldwide.

papers.title: Climate change 2021: The physical science basis
papers.authors: IPCC Working Group I
papers.abstract: The Working Group I contribution to the Sixth Assessment Report addresses the most up-to-date physical understanding of the climate system and climate change, bringing together the latest advances in climate science from over 200 authors.

papers.title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
papers.authors: Jumper J, Evans R, Pritzel A, et al.
papers.abstract: AlphaFold achieves atomic accuracy in protein structure prediction, solving a 50-year-old grand challenge in biology. The method uses a novel deep learning architecture incorporating evolutionary features and structure-based representations.

papers.title: Effect of Colchicine on Cardiovascular Outcomes in Patients with Chronic Coronary Disease
papers.authors: Nidorf SM, Fiolet ATL, Mosterd A, et al.
papers.abstract: In the LoDoCo2 trial, colchicine at a dose of 0.5 mg daily led to a significantly lower risk of cardiovascular events than placebo in patients with chronic coronary disease.

papers.title: Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2020 awarded to CRISPR pioneers Doudna and Charpentier
papers.authors: Nobel Prize Organization
papers.abstract: Emmanuelle Charpentier and Jennifer A. Doudna have been awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2020 for the development of a method for genome editing using CRISPR-Cas9.

papers.title: An mRNA Vaccine against SARS-CoV-2 - Preliminary Report
papers.authors: Jackson LA, Anderson EJ, Rouphael NG, et al.
papers.abstract: This landmark study describes the first clinical trial of an mRNA vaccine (BNT162b1) against SARS-CoV-2, demonstrating safety and robust immunogenicity, paving the way for the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine.

papers.title: A Trial of Liraglutide for Obesity in Adolescents
papers.authors: Kelly AS, Auerbach P, Barrientos-Perez M, et al.
papers.abstract: In this randomized trial, liraglutide plus lifestyle therapy resulted in a significantly greater reduction in BMI than placebo plus lifestyle therapy among adolescents with obesity.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.authors: Devlin J, Chang MW, Lee K, Toutanova K
papers.abstract: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text.

papers.title: Single-cell transcriptomics of 20 mouse organs creates a Tabula Muris
papers.authors: Tabula Muris Consortium
papers.abstract: We have created a compendium of single-cell transcriptomic data from the model organism Mus musculus comprising more than 100,000 cells from 20 organs and tissues, representing a new resource for cell biology.

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.authors: Vaswani A, Shazeer N, Parmar N, et al.
papers.abstract: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. This has become the foundation of modern NLP and large language models including GPT and BERT.

papers.title: Global warming and recurrent mass bleaching of corals
papers.authors: Hughes TP, Kerry JT, Alvarez-Noriega M, et al.
papers.abstract: During 2015-2016, record temperatures triggered a pan-tropical episode of coral bleaching. The frequency of severe bleaching events has increased dramatically, threatening the future of coral reefs worldwide.

papers.title: Semaglutide and Cardiovascular Outcomes in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes
papers.authors: Marso SP, Bain SC, Consoli A, et al.
papers.abstract: The SUSTAIN-6 trial demonstrated that semaglutide, a GLP-1 receptor agonist, significantly reduced the risk of cardiovascular events in patients with type 2 diabetes at high cardiovascular risk.

papers.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
papers.authors: He K, Zhang X, Ren S, Sun J
papers.abstract: We present a residual learning framework to ease the training of networks that are substantially deeper than those used previously. Our residual networks (ResNet) won the ImageNet 2015 competition with 152 layers.

papers.title: The Paris Agreement and the New Logic of International Climate Politics
papers.authors: Falkner R
papers.abstract: The 2015 Paris Agreement represents a turning point in international climate governance, introducing a hybrid architecture of binding and non-binding elements, and a new logic of norm evolution in global environmental politics.

papers.title: Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search
papers.authors: Silver D, Huang A, Maddison CJ, et al.
papers.abstract: We describe the AlphaGo program that defeated the human European Go champion and the world champion Lee Sedol. AlphaGo combines deep neural networks with Monte Carlo tree search using policy and value networks.

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Networks
papers.authors: Goodfellow IJ, Pouget-Abadie J, Mirza M, et al.
papers.abstract: We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data.

papers.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
papers.authors: Krizhevsky A, Sutskever I, Hinton GE
papers.abstract: We trained a large, deep convolutional neural network to classify the 1.2 million high-resolution images in the ImageNet LSVRC-2010 contest into the 1000 different classes. Our model (AlexNet) achieved top-1 and top-5 error rates of 37.5% and 17.0%.

papers.title: A Reconciled Estimate of Ice-Sheet Mass Balance
papers.authors: Shepherd A, Ivins ER, Geruo A, et al.
papers.abstract: We combined an ensemble of satellite altimetry, interferometry, and gravimetry data sets to estimate the mass balance of Earth's polar ice sheets, finding significant ice loss contributing to sea-level rise.

papers.title: A Programmable Dual-RNA-Guided DNA Endonuclease in Adaptive Bacterial Immunity
papers.authors: Jinek M, Chylinski K, Fonfara I, et al.
papers.abstract: We report the programmable dual-RNA-guided DNA endonuclease Cas9 from Streptococcus pyogenes. Our findings reveal a family of endonucleases that can be directed to cut specific DNA sequences, enabling genome engineering applications.

papers.title: The Human Genome Project: A Perspective
papers.authors: Green ED, Guyer MS, National Human Genome Research Institute
papers.abstract: A retrospective on the Human Genome Project, describing the strategies, technologies, and lessons learned from sequencing the first complete human reference genome, and its transformative impact on biomedical research.

papers.title: A safe operating space for humanity
papers.authors: Rockstrom J, Steffen W, Noone K, et al.
papers.abstract: We propose a framework based on 'planetary boundaries' that defines a safe operating space for humanity with respect to the Earth system and are associated with the planet's biophysical subsystems or processes.

papers.title: Climate Change 2007: Synthesis Report
papers.authors: IPCC Core Writing Team
papers.abstract: The Synthesis Report of the IPCC Fourth Assessment Report integrates findings from the three Working Groups on the physical science basis, impacts and adaptation, and mitigation of climate change.

papers.title: Initial sequence of the chimpanzee genome and comparison with the human genome
papers.authors: Chimpanzee Sequencing and Analysis Consortium
papers.abstract: We present a draft genome sequence of the common chimpanzee (Pan troglodytes). Through comparison with the human genome, we have generated a largely complete catalogue of genetic differences between humans and chimpanzees.

papers.title: Cardiac-Resynchronization Therapy with or without an Implantable Defibrillator in Advanced Chronic Heart Failure
papers.authors: Bristow MR, Saxon LA, Boehmer J, et al.
papers.abstract: In the COMPANION trial, cardiac-resynchronization therapy (CRT) with or without a defibrillator reduced the risk of death and hospitalization from heart failure in patients with advanced heart failure and intraventricular conduction delays.

papers.title: Reduction in the Incidence of Type 2 Diabetes with Lifestyle Intervention or Metformin
papers.authors: Diabetes Prevention Program Research Group
papers.abstract: The Diabetes Prevention Program is a randomized clinical trial to determine whether diet and exercise or metformin can prevent or delay the onset of type 2 diabetes in persons with impaired glucose tolerance.

papers.title: The sequence of the human genome
papers.authors: Venter JC, Adams MD, Myers EW, et al.
papers.abstract: A report from Celera Genomics on the draft sequence of the human genome generated using whole-genome shotgun sequencing, providing an alternative to the public Human Genome Project approach.
